definition: One of the four standard TEE imaging levels; the probe tip is positioned in the esophagus at the level of the heart, behind the left atrium.
Imaging plane orientation relative to a structure's long axis: SAX cuts across it (cross-section), LAX runs along it.
A flow-convergence method for quantifying regurgitant or stenotic flow by measuring the radius of a hemispheric isovelocity shell proximal to the orifice.
The smallest cross-sectional area of a regurgitant jet, used to quantify the severity of valvular regurgitation.
The average of instantaneous pressure gradients across the aortic valve during systole, derived from the continuous-wave Doppler velocity trace via the Bernoulli equation.
A principle stating that stroke volume through the LVOT must equal stroke volume through the aortic valve; used to calculate aortic valve area.
